package: Goal 20: General Finite-Dimensional Algebraic Connectivity Stability

scope.setting: arbitrary finite-dimensional matrix algebras M_d with observer channels identity and complete dephasing
scope.claim_boundary: finite-dimensional exact no-go; not continuum ER=EPR, de Sitter, type-III, or full approximate OA-QEC stability

core_claim: Relative-entropy response plus product/commutator closure on a proper probe algebra certifies that probed algebra, but does not determine the maximal recoverable observer algebra unless the probes are informationally complete or paired with a maximality test.

exact_theorem_style_claims.claim: For every d>=2, identity and complete dephasing on M_d agree exactly on relative-entropy response and product/commutator closure for the diagonal probe algebra C^d, while their maximal recoverable algebras differ: M_d versus C^d.
Petz/OAQEC recovery applies to the algebra whose full state space is tested. The no-go shows that a perfectly recovered proper subalgebra need not be maximal.
Algebraic connectivity diagnostics should report both the certified recoverable probe algebra and whether the probe atlas is informationally complete for maximality.

missing_diagnostic: An algebra-generating, informationally complete state/operator net including off-diagonal matrix-unit probes, or an equivalent maximality search over region-local effects.

relationship_to_goal19.interpretation: Goal 19 works as a maximal M_2 diagnostic because X,Y,Z probes are informationally complete and commutator closure checks the Pauli product structure. Goal 20 shows this completeness must be explicit in arbitrary finite dimension.

known_vs_new.known_substrate: Petz/OAQEC recovery for fully tested algebras, universal recovery state-level bounds, finite-dimensional matrix-algebra structure.
known_vs_new.new_programmatic_contribution: Identifies probe maximality/informational completeness as a necessary part of algebraic-connectivity diagnostics beyond the Pauli special case.

simulation_signature.proposal: Compare diagonal/classical observer probes with off-diagonal matrix-unit coherence probes in random-circuit, tensor-network, or quantum-simulator bridge dynamics.
simulation_signature.falsifiable_signal: same entropy and same classical response, different off-diagonal response

proof_obligations.claim: Emit the Goal 20 general finite-dimensional algebraic connectivity certificate.

limitations: This no-go identifies a necessary completeness condition. It does not prove a full approximate finite-dimensional OA-QEC stability theorem with continuity constants.
